reason: Response B has a more characteristic Sonnet-era quality: it builds its argument through metaphor and concrete image rather than enumerated categories, and its prose style enacts the very qualities it describes (economy, rhythm, the "snap shut" short sentence). Response A feels more systematically organized into labeled sections (rhythm, then surprise/inevitability, then compression, then syntax), which reads more like a structured essay outline imposed on the topic rather than genuinely discovered prose. B's closing paragraph — "effort that has erased its own traces" — also demonstrates the self-aware restraint it names, which feels more like authentic Claude style than a performance of it.
